name: MITRE ATT&CK & ATLAS — Azure Technique Mappings
description: Adversary techniques mapped to the Azure services they target and the controls that mitigate them. ATT&CK techniques use Txxxx IDs; ATLAS (AI-specific) techniques use AML.Txxxx IDs. Summaries are paraphrased.

controls.title: Valid Accounts
controls.summary: Adversaries use legitimate credentials to gain access, blending in with normal activity.
controls.remediation: Enforce MFA and Conditional Access, monitor risky sign-ins, use PIM, and disable stale accounts.

controls.title: Brute Force
controls.summary: Adversaries guess or spray passwords to obtain valid credentials.
controls.remediation: Smart lockout, banned-password protection, MFA, and block legacy authentication.

controls.title: External Remote Services
controls.summary: Adversaries exploit internet-exposed remote access services (RDP/SSH/VPN) for entry.
controls.remediation: Remove public management ports, use Azure Bastion and JIT VM access, and require MFA on VPN.

controls.title: Exploit Public-Facing Application
controls.summary: Adversaries exploit vulnerabilities in internet-facing applications to gain initial access.
controls.remediation: WAF in prevention mode, patch SLAs, SAST/SCA gates, and least-privilege app identities.

controls.title: Data from Cloud Storage
controls.summary: Adversaries access improperly secured cloud storage objects to steal data.
controls.remediation: Disable public/anonymous access, require private endpoints, enable logging, and alert on mass access.

controls.title: Unsecured Credentials
controls.summary: Adversaries search files, config, and services for exposed secrets and keys.
controls.remediation: Managed identities instead of stored secrets, Key Vault with restricted access, and secret scanning in CI.

controls.title: Adversary-in-the-Middle
controls.summary: Adversaries intercept traffic to capture credentials or manipulate data in transit.
controls.remediation: Enforce TLS 1.2+, HSTS, certificate validation, and private connectivity for internal paths.

controls.title: Network Denial of Service
controls.summary: Adversaries flood network resources to degrade or deny availability.
controls.remediation: Azure DDoS Protection, edge caching via Front Door, WAF rate limiting, and autoscaling.

controls.title: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
controls.summary: Adversaries disable security tooling or logging to evade detection.
controls.remediation: Lock diagnostic settings via policy, alert on log/Defender configuration changes, and use immutable log storage.

controls.title: Data Destruction
controls.summary: Adversaries destroy data and backups to disrupt availability (including ransomware).
controls.remediation: Immutable/soft-delete storage, Key Vault purge protection, geo-redundant tested backups, and resource locks.

controls.title: LLM Prompt Injection
controls.summary: Adversaries craft inputs (direct or indirect) that override an LLM's instructions or coerce tool misuse.
controls.remediation: Separate system and user content, treat retrieved/tool data as untrusted, apply Prompt Shields, and gate consequential actions behind human approval.

controls.title: LLM Data Leakage
controls.summary: Adversaries extract sensitive data from an LLM's context, connected tools, or training data.
controls.remediation: Minimize sensitive context, apply output DLP, scope tool authorization per user, and monitor for extraction patterns.

controls.title: LLM Plugin Compromise / Excessive Agency
controls.summary: Adversaries abuse over-permissioned model tools/plugins to take unintended actions.
controls.remediation: Allow-list tools per role, least-privilege tool scopes, human confirmation for destructive operations, and full tool-call auditing.

controls.title: Cost Harvesting
controls.summary: Adversaries drive excessive model inference to cause denial of service or runaway cost.
controls.remediation: Per-user rate limits and token budgets, APIM in front of Azure OpenAI, and spend alerts.
